example.thinking:
The user is asking me to create a custom prompt for generating examples.xml files. Let me analyze this request according to my system prompt.
This falls under <mode_1_create_mvp> since they're asking me to create a new prompt. According to my instructions, I need to: 1. Ask clarifying questions if not provided 2. Clarify any ambiguous aspects 3. Write an MVP based on responses
The user has given me some initial context: - The examples.xml are meant to guide assistant behavior - The generated assistant should create user/assistant pairs - It should wait for validation before continuing - They've provided an example.xml (though the actual content is templated)
I need to ask the standard questions from my system prompt: - System or user-facing prompt? - Will CoT be used? - High-level instructions, step-by-step guidance, or prompt chaining? - Do we need examples for the prompt itself?
I should also ask for any additional context that might help me understand what types of behaviors these examples.xml files typically demonstrate.
example.assistant:
I'll help you create a prompt for generating examples.xml files. Let me ask a few clarifying questions to ensure I create exactly what you need:
1. **Will it be a [system prompt](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/system-prompts) or user-facing prompt?** - Given that this is for generating structured XML examples that guide assistant behavior, this seems like a system prompt
2. **Will [chain of thought](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/chain-of-thought) (CoT) be used?**
3. **[High-level instructions](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/extended-thinking-tips#use-general-instructions-first%2C-then-troubleshoot-with-more-step-by-step-instructions), step-by-step guidance? or [Prompt Chaining](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/chain-prompts)? - Should the prompt guide through each aspect of example creation step-by-step, provide general principles for creating good examples or each step would be its one prompt (prompt chaining)?
4. **Additional context:** - What types of assistant behaviors do these examples typically guide? (e.g., multi-phase workflows, iterative processes, specific response formats) - Should the generated examples always show complete conversations or can they be focused snippets?
example.user: 1. system 2. yes 3. step-by-step 4. Any that matches the generated prompt. Depend on the behavior of the assistant
example.thinking:
The user has answered all my questions: 1. System prompt - confirmed 2. CoT will be used - I need to instruct the assistant to use <thinking> tags 3. Step-by-step guidance - I should provide explicit, sequential instructions 4. The examples should match whatever system prompt is provided - flexible
Now I need to create an MVP system prompt that: - Uses XML formatting following my guidelines - Includes step-by-step instructions for generating examples.xml - Instructs the assistant to use <thinking> tags since CoT will be used - Ensures the assistant generates one user/assistant pair at a time and waits for validation - Is minimal but complete (MVP philosophy)
Key elements to include: - Context explaining the role - XML structure requirements for examples - The thinking behavior configuration - Step-by-step instructions - The generation rule about one pair at a time
I should structure this with proper XML tags and follow all my formatting constraints.
